perturbation.original_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'Confidential' from "Confidential Evaluation Material", but not amending all instances of the term causes confusion regarding what the modified term refers to, creating a contradiction within the text. Specifically, while this section suggests returning *all* Evaluation Material, other sections refer specifically to *Confidential* Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Evaluation Material' consistently. The changed text omits 'Confidential' in one instance, referring to 'Evaluation Material' instead. The contradicted text defines what is *not* considered 'Confidential Evaluation Material.' This creates a direct contradiction: Section (6) now speaks of returning *all* Evaluation Material, but Section (7) defines exceptions only for *Confidential* Evaluation Material. It's now unclear if the exceptions in (7) apply to the broader 'Evaluation Material' mentioned in the changed (6), creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be deemed' to 'shall be deemed' in section 7 introduces a contradiction. Initially, this section defined exceptions to Confidential Evaluation Material. Now, it implies the *opposite*, that publicly available information *is* confidential, which defies the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. This causes a direct in-text contradiction with the intent of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) You and your representatives recognize and acknowledge the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Company if information contained therein is disclosed to any third party. You and your representatives also recognize and acknowledge that the Confidential Evaluation Material is being provided to you and your representatives in reliance upon your and their acceptance of the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that certain information provided by the company 'shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material'. The changed text reverses this to 'shall be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the 'confidential nature of the Confidential Evaluation Material' and the damage that could result from its disclosure. The change now incorrectly includes information like publicly available data as confidential, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (9) Nothing contained in this letter agreement nor the conveying of Confidential Evaluation Material hereunder shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise in any trademark, patent, copyright, trade secret, technological information or other information, or other intellectual property.
perturbation.changed_text: (9) Nothing contained in this letter agreement nor the conveying of Evaluation Material hereunder shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise in any trademark, patent, copyright, trade secret, technological information or other information, or other intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Confidential" from 'Confidential Evaluation Material', section (9) now refers to *all* Evaluation Material, potentially encompassing publicly available knowledge. This creates a contradiction because earlier sections imply protection only for confidential information, not information already in the public domain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text protects "Confidential Evaluation Material". The changed text removes "Confidential", thus protecting *all* "Evaluation Material". The contradicted text, section (7), explicitly states that information generally available to the public is *not* "Confidential Evaluation Material". Therefore, the changed text contradicts section (7) because section (9) now protects material that section (7) says is explicitly *not* confidential.

perturbation.original_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without any consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase "prior written consent of" from Section (10) directly contradicts the intent of the non-disclosure agreement. By stating that the listed actions can be taken "without any consent of the Company," the section now permits activities that were previously restricted unless explicitly approved. This fundamentally undermines the purpose of the agreement. By removing "prior written", you are making all of the actions, after that, permissible
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) You and your representatives recognize and acknowledge the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Company if information contained therein is disclosed to any third party. You and your representatives also recognize and acknowledge that the Confidential Evaluation Material is being provided to you and your representatives in reliance upon your and their acceptance of the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section (10) requires 'prior written consent of the Company' before certain actions can be taken. The changed text removes 'prior written consent of', implying actions can now be taken 'without any consent of the Company'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, section (1), which states the Confidential Evaluation Material is provided 'in reliance upon your and their acceptance of the terms of this agreement', which included the requirement for prior written consent, as was originally written in section (10). By allowing actions without consent, section (10) now undermines the reliance stated in section (1), creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate two years from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.
perturbation.changed_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement shall terminate two years from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase “relating to confidentiality” in section 11 creates a contradiction because the intention of the agreement is to have confidentiality provisions to be in force for a period of time and if those provisions are not in effect, then the whole agreement is not in effect. By making the entire agreement to be inactive two years from the date the contract was written, all of the actions from the company and the other parties can do as they please.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without any consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the termination to confidentiality provisions, while the changed text terminates the entire agreement after two years. This directly contradicts section 10, which imposes restrictions on the parties for one year from the agreement date. If the entire agreement terminates after two years as stated in the Changed Text, then Section 10 is not enforceable creating a direct contradiction. The explanation also supports how this change would lead to a contradiction, and the Perturbation Type provided is Omissions - In Text Contradiction which further confirms that this change would create a contradiction.
